---
title: "Boxplots — Yogurt qPCR Outcomes"
format: html
editor: source
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

> Using `ggboxplot()` + `stat_compare_means()` to compare qPCR bacterial levels between study arms, then `facet_wrap()` to screen all 3 outcomes at once.

## Setup

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(ggpubr)

# ── Read sample IDs (links sample_id ↔ pid ↔ arm) ──
samples <- read_csv(
  here("data/group_a_yogurt/00_sample_ids_yogurt.csv"),
  col_types = cols(pid = col_character())
)

# ── Read qPCR results ──
qpcr <- read_csv(
  here("data/group_a_yogurt/02_qpcr_results_yogurt.csv"),
  col_types = cols(sample_id = col_character())
)

# ── Join to get arm ──
df <- samples |>
  select(sample_id, arm) |>
  inner_join(qpcr, by = "sample_id")

glimpse(df)
```

## 1 — Single outcome: total bacteria

```{r single}
ggboxplot(
  df,
  x = "arm",
  y = "qpcr_bacteria",
  color = "arm",
  palette = "jco",
  add = "jitter"
) +
  stat_compare_means() +                        # default: Wilcoxon test
  labs(
    title = "Total bacteria by study arm",
    x = "Arm",
    y = "Total bacteria (copies/mL)"
  )
```

## 2 — Screen all 3 outcomes with `facet_wrap()`

Pivot the data longer so each row is one sample × one bacterial target, then facet.

```{r facet}
df |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(qpcr_bacteria, qpcr_crispatus, qpcr_iners),
    names_to = "bacteria",
    values_to = "copies"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    bacteria = recode(bacteria,
      qpcr_bacteria  = "Total bacteria",
      qpcr_crispatus = "L. crispatus",
      qpcr_iners     = "L. iners"
    )
  ) |>
  ggboxplot(
    x = "arm",
    y = "copies",
    color = "arm",
    palette = "jco",
    add = "jitter"
  ) +
  stat_compare_means() +
  facet_wrap(~ bacteria, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "qPCR outcomes by study arm",
    x = "Arm",
    y = "Copies/mL"
  )
```
